title: OWASP Agentic Application Top 10 — ADAM + AGT Light Coverage Map
description: Machine-readable mapping of OWASP Agentic Application Top 10 (December 2025) to ADAM architectural coverage and AGT Light runtime enforcement. Version 1.0, April 2026.
coverage_posture: ADAM provides complete architectural coverage of all 10 risks. AGT Light adds runtime enforcement at the execution boundary. Together: defense in depth.

risks.name: Excessive Agency
risks.description: Agent given too many permissions or capabilities beyond what its task requires

components: BOSS Score: Sovereignty Action dimension (weight 4.0) scores every action's authority scope
Exception Economy: autonomy budgets cap cumulative scope expansion
CORE Engine: tools_authorized list per agent vertex in CORE Graph
81-Agent Mesh: bounded authority containers per agent class

agt_runtime_enforcement.mechanism: Agent OS capability sandboxing blocks tool calls outside tools_authorized list at <0.1ms. Agent Runtime execution rings prevent cross-ring privilege escalation architecturally.

risks.combined_posture: Architectural constraint (BOSS + CORE Engine) + Runtime enforcement (Agent OS capability sandboxing) + Privilege containment (Agent Runtime rings)

risks.name: Unrestricted Autonomy
risks.description: Agent permitted to take actions indefinitely without human oversight or stopping conditions

components: BOSS Score SOAP-to-OHSHAT escalation tiers: autonomy is tiered, not binary
Autonomy budgets: consumable resources that enforce stopping conditions
Exception Economy: 15% tolerance window triggers mandatory review
5-Director Constitution: Director approval required at HIGH (51-75) tier

agt_runtime_enforcement.mechanism: Agent OS enforces BOSS tier gates at invocation boundary. Agent Runtime kill switch provides emergency stopping. Agent SRE error budgets enforce circuit breakers when operational limits are exceeded.
agt_runtime_enforcement.deployment_config: exception-economy-config.yaml: escalation_tiers + agt_trust_thresholds; agent-slo-config.yaml: circuit breaker thresholds

risks.combined_posture: Tiered escalation (BOSS SOAP-to-OHSHAT) + Economic stopping condition (autonomy budgets) + Runtime circuit breakers (Agent SRE) + Emergency shutdown (Agent Runtime kill switch)

risks.name: Goal Hijacking
risks.description: Malicious content in agent's context manipulates agent to pursue attacker's goals

adam_architectural_coverage.primary_plane: Policy & Risk Plane + Security & Trust Plane
components: Separation of intelligence from execution capability: agents cannot self-modify their authority scope
CORE Engine validates every intent against doctrine graph before execution
Policy & Risk Plane evaluates action against CORE Engine at every orchestration step
BOSS Doctrinal Alignment dimension: measures deviation from authorized intent

agt_runtime_enforcement.mechanism: Agent OS semantic intent classifier detects instruction injection at the invocation boundary before tool execution. Policy evaluation blocks actions that deviate from the intent declared in the originating Intent Object.

risks.combined_posture: Architectural intent validation (CORE Engine doctrine check) + Runtime semantic classification (Agent OS) + Evidence chain (Flight Recorder captures all intent deviations)

risks.name: Tool Misuse
risks.description: Agent uses available tools in unintended ways or invokes unauthorized tools

components: Cryptographic Authorization Vault: high-risk tool invocations require vault-issued authorization
Policy-bounded invocation contracts: per-agent, per-context tool authorization
MCP-Ready Control Surfaces: signed tool contracts for external integrations
CORE Engine tools_authorized per agent vertex

agt_runtime_enforcement.mechanism: Agent OS capability sandboxing blocks invocations not in the authorized tool list. Agent Marketplace verifies Ed25519-signed manifests for all external tool/plugin invocations before execution.

risks.combined_posture: Vault-based authorization (Cryptographic Authorization Vault) + Runtime capability sandboxing (Agent OS) + Supply chain integrity (Agent Marketplace signing)

risks.name: Identity and Access Compromise
risks.description: Agent identity spoofed or access controls bypassed, enabling unauthorized actions

components: Security & Trust Plane: verifiable identity requirement for all agents
No anonymous execution permitted anywhere in the 81-Agent Mesh
Scoped permissions per agent: authority scope defined in CORE Graph Agent vertex
mTLS enforced between all agent pods via Istio

agt_runtime_enforcement.mechanism: Agent Mesh issues W3C DID-based identity (Ed25519) to every agent at startup. IATP verifies identity and trust score on every inter-agent communication. Compromised identities are revoked via DID revocation registry without service interruption.
agt_runtime_enforcement.deployment_config: agt-mesh-identity-bootstrap Job + agentMesh values: didMethod, keyVaultKeyRef

risks.combined_posture: Architectural identity requirement (Security & Trust Plane) + Cryptographic non-forgeability (Agent Mesh DIDs + Ed25519) + Continuous trust re-evaluation (IATP 0-1000 score)

risks.name: Uncontrolled Delegation
risks.description: Agent delegates authority to other agents without proper authorization checks

adam_architectural_coverage.primary_plane: Security & Trust Plane + Agent Mesh & Orchestration Fabric
components: Authority boundaries enforced by CORE Engine: delegation scope defined per agent
Governor Agent approval gates required for all cross-domain delegation
Cryptographic Authorization Vault: delegation authority stored as smart-contract governed grants
A2A Protocol: delegation messages carry cryptographic proof of authorization

agt_runtime_enforcement.mechanism: Agent Mesh IATP trust scoring blocks delegation to agents with trust score below the delegation threshold. Delegation without a valid trust chain is rejected at the Agent Mesh layer before reaching the target agent.

risks.combined_posture: Architectural delegation boundaries (CORE Engine + Authority Vault) + Runtime trust chain validation (Agent Mesh IATP) + Evidence capture (Flight Recorder delegation events)

risks.name: Insufficient Monitoring and Logging
risks.description: Insufficient visibility into agent actions, preventing detection of anomalies or attacks

components: Flight Recorder: hash-chained, tamper-evident, cryptographically signed entries
7-year WORM retention with Azure Confidential Ledger tamper anchoring
Self-Audit Agent: continuously validates evidence chain integrity
BOSS Score captured for every action in Azure Data Explorer time-series

agt_runtime_enforcement.mechanism: Agent SRE provides quantitative SLO monitoring, error budget tracking, and structured telemetry for all agent operations. RGI-04 adapter forwards all AGT telemetry to the Flight Recorder in ADAM's canonical evidence format.
agt_runtime_enforcement.deployment_config: rgiAdapter.enabled: true + agent-slo-config.yaml + Flight Recorder infrastructure (Section 6.2 of Deployment Spec)

risks.combined_posture: Constitutional evidence (Flight Recorder — not operational telemetry) + Quantitative SRE monitoring (Agent SRE) + Telemetry bridge (RGI-04 adapter)

risks.name: Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
risks.description: Malicious or compromised tools, models, or plugins introduced into the agent's execution environment

components: MCP-Ready Control Surfaces: signed tool contracts for external integrations
Cryptographic Authorization Vault: external tool authorization as vault-governed grants
Interoperability Plane: security posture for all external integration points
CORE Engine tools_authorized list: whitelist enforcement at agent level

agt_runtime_enforcement.mechanism: Agent Marketplace enforces Ed25519 manifest signing for all external tools and plugins. Attestation verification is performed before any tool invocation. Revocation registry is checked hourly; real-time revocation check on BOSS HIGH+ actions.
agt_runtime_enforcement.deployment_config: agentMarketplace values: requireSignedManifests: true, trustedPublisherKeys, revocationCheckInterval

risks.combined_posture: Architectural tool governance (MCP signed contracts + Auth Vault) + Runtime manifest verification (Agent Marketplace) + Revocation infrastructure (DID revocation registry)

risks.name: Operational Disruption
risks.description: Agent actions cause unintended disruptions to production systems or downstream services

components: Stability & Drift Plane: continuous monitoring of agent operational deviation
Operational Continuity Agent: maintains service continuity under failure conditions
Recovery & Rollback Hook (Part X of main book): native idempotency and compensation
Exception Economy: non-idempotent action penalties create economic disincentive for disruptive patterns

agt_runtime_enforcement.mechanism: Agent SRE circuit breakers trip automatically when downstream agents/services degrade, routing to fallback behavior. Agent Runtime saga orchestration wraps all operations with compensating transactions for rollback. Chaos engineering validates resilience assumptions pre-production.
agt_runtime_enforcement.deployment_config: agentRuntime values: sagaCompensationTimeout + agentSre values: errorBudgetWindowDays, sloConfigMapRef

risks.combined_posture: Architectural resilience design (Stability & Drift Plane + Recovery Hook) + Economic disruption disincentive (non-idempotent penalties) + Runtime circuit breakers and saga compensation (Agent SRE + Agent Runtime)

risks.name: Misalignment and Trust Erosion
risks.description: Agent behavior gradually drifts from intended objectives, eroding trust through accumulated misalignment

adam_architectural_coverage.primary_plane: Stability & Drift Plane + Policy & Risk Plane
components: CORE Engine drift detection: DriftRecord vertices track measured divergence from doctrine
Autonomy Stability Agent: continuously evaluates and reports alignment scores
BOSS Doctrinal Alignment dimension (weight 3.0): scores every action's doctrine fit
Doctrine versioning: every change is tagged, signed, and evidenced in Flight Recorder

agt_runtime_enforcement.mechanism: Agent Lightning (optional, for RL-enabled deployments) enforces training boundaries preventing reinforcement learning from optimizing toward misaligned reward signals. RL boundary policy set to 'strict' prevents value drift during model updates.
agt_runtime_enforcement.deployment_config: agentLightning values: enabled (set true for RL deployments), rlBoundaryPolicy: strict
agt_runtime_enforcement.optional_note: Agent Lightning is only required for deployments with RL-trained agents. ADAM's drift detection (CORE Engine + Autonomy Stability Agent) operates independently of Agent Lightning.

risks.combined_posture: Architectural drift measurement (CORE Engine DriftRecord + BOSS Doctrinal Alignment) + Proactive detection (Autonomy Stability Agent) + RL boundary enforcement (Agent Lightning, optional)
